channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Reviewing the Vance-Walz vice-presidential debate
item.description: Wednesday at 9 a.m., MPR News host Angela Davis talks with political analysts about Tuesday night’s vice-presidential debate between Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Walz and Vance will meet in their first and possibly only vice presidential debate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tim Walz and JD Vance will meet for their first and possibly only vice presidential debate Tuesday, in what could be the last debate for both campaigns to argue their case before the election.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris, Trump woo Gen Z voters in critical swing states
item.description: The Harris and Trump campaigns are stepping up their outreach to Generation Z voters in critical battlegrounds, targeting a voting bloc expected to play a key role in a tight White House race where any marginal advantage gained could make the difference.
item.dc:creator: Yash Roy, NewsNation

item.title: Gillibrand vs. Sapraicone: How to watch the Race for US Senate forum
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand and Republican candidate Mike Sapraicone will go head to head in a Senate forum airing on PIX11 News Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Fox News Power Rankings: Trump maintains lead on 2 top issues ahead of VP debate
item.description: The latest Fox News Power Rankings show whether Vice President Kamala Harris or former President Donald Trump leads on the top issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 possible October surprises that could roil the Trump-Harris race
item.description: The 2024 presidential race has seen enough shocking events transpire in a few short months to fill an entire calendar year, but that doesn’t mean there won’t be a much-talked about October surprise looming in the final weeks of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats sue over controversial Georgia voting rule
item.description: Democrats filed a lawsuit Monday looking to block a controversial rule requiring ballots to be hand-counted by local precincts in Georgia, arguing it violated Georgia law and called it “improper.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris, Trump shift plans after Hurricane Helene's destruction
item.description: Hurricane Helene is shifting the presidential candidates’ plans this week. Democratic nominee Kamala Harris is cutting short a campaign visit to Las Vegas to return to Washington for briefings. Republican candidate Donald Trump is heading to Georgia to see the storm’s impact.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump slams US response to Helene. His own disaster-response record is marked by politics
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Donald Trump criticized the Biden administration’s response to the widespread devastation caused by Hurricane Helene, even as his supporters have called for cuts to federal agencies that warn of weather disasters and deliver relief to hard-hit communities.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New NY poll shows Gillibrand leading, Hochul approval sagging
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York Democrat Kirsten Gillibrand has a 17-point lead as she seeks her third full term in the U.S. Senate, an exclusive poll from PIX11, The Hill and Emerson College shows. But it was not great news for all Democrats -- especially Gov. Kathy Hochul.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: The Pentagon says it wants to prevent Iran from spreading the conflict in the Middle East
item.description: After Israel killed Hezbollah’s leader, the U.S. says it wants to stop Iran from “exploiting the situation or expanding the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Democrats fight to hang on to Nevada in Harris-Trump race
item.description: Democrats are fighting tooth and nail to keep Nevada from slipping away from them this cycle after narrowly winning it in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tim Walz’s experience in China could help him as veep, but he barely mentions it as a candidate
item.description: When Gov. Tim Walz first ran for Congress in 2006, his campaign proudly touted his extensive experience in China. In fact, earlier in his political career, he occasionally exaggerated it. But now that he’s running for vice president, Walz barely mentions China.
item.dc:creator: Forest Hunt, Caspar von Au, and Curtis Gilbert, MPR News

item.title: Key Senate race in Arizona could hinge on voters who back Trump and the Democratic candidate
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — If Ruben Gallego is going to give Democrats their fourth straight U.S. Senate victory in Arizona, he's probably going to need support from an unlikely group: Donald Trump voters.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance criticized an infrastructure law as a candidate then embraced it as a senator
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As he campaigned for the Senate two years ago, JD Vance harshly criticized a bipartisan 2021 law to invest more than $1 trillion in America’s crumbling infrastructure, calling it a “huge mistake” shaped by Democrats who want to spend big taxpayer dollars on “really crazy stuff.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 58 percent favor electing presidents based on popular vote: Gallup
item.description: The Gallup survey found that 58 percent of Americans are in favor of making the change, while 39 percent still prefer the Electoral College system.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Florida congressional candidate charged for threatening to kill opponent
item.description: William Robert Braddock III of St. Petersburg, Fla., was charged with one count of interstate transmission of a true threat to injure another person. He could face a maximum of five years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers race to avoid farm bill disaster
item.description: The stopgap farm bill is set to expire next week — and the congressional coalition that the massive legislation has relied on for half a century is on the verge of breaking down.
item.dc:creator: Saul Elbein, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he'll speak with Israeli leader, vowing all-out war in the Middle East must be avoided
item.description: DOVER AIR FORCE BASE, Del. (AP) — President Joe Biden said Sunday he would speak with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, and believes that an all-out war in the Middle East must be avoided. “It has to be," Biden told reporters as he boarded Air Force One for Washington.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Understanding the cat lore of the 2024 presidential election
item.description: The “cat lady” tagline took off this year as an insult and an accolade — now it’s a mainstay in the presidential campaigns and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Pregnancy-related prosecutions hit high first year post-Roe: Study
item.description: Advocacy group Pregnancy Justice compiled pregnancy-related prosecutions between June 2022 and June 2023.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Fact-checking claims Harris and Trump have made about the border
item.description: NewsNation fact-checked these claims, revealing a mix of truths, exaggerations, and falsehoods from both candidates.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz CBS debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams issues order changing First Deputy Mayor succession
item.description: dams outlined that in the event the First Deputy Mayor is unable to fulfill the functions of the mayor, the Deputy Mayor for Health and Human Services would be next in line.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation

item.title: Leading the charge: Red Lake unveils new electric school buses during ribbon-cutting ceremony
item.description: Red Lake celebrated the acquisition of two electric school buses Friday. The school district is one of the first in Minnesota to use such vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Politics Friday: Poll shows Harris with narrow Minnesota lead, but what else can we glean from it?
item.description: With new candidates on the Democratic ticket and former president Donald Trump atop the Republican ticket for a third time, a new Minnesota poll shows Kamala Harris with an edge. Coming up Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst talks with Minnesota journalists about the poll results.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, Ellie Roth, and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: On ballot herself, Klobuchar devotes campaign time and resources to lifting other DFLers
item.description: Minnesota Democrats are banking on Sen. Amy Klobuchar to boost their ticket around the state even though she hasn’t had to expend significant effort in her own campaign for another term.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Nevada's Horsford calls to 'end hate' following Republican congressman's tweet calling Haitians 'thugs'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Rep. Steven Horsford called for the censure of a Republican U.S. House member after that congressman posted and then deleted a racist tweet.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Secret Service agent placed on leave after 'misconduct allegation' with Harris staffer
item.description: The U.S. Secret Service recently notified Vice President Kamala Harris’ office about an incident involving one of its agents and a “misconduct allegation,” according to spokespeople for both the USSS and the vice president's office.
item.dc:creator: Monica Alba and Kelly O'Donnell, WFLA, NBC
